     The Numbers Business Report

Aquamarine (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,597,342Details
International Box Office $4,381,611
Worldwide Box Office $22,978,953
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $31,720,234
Total $31,720,234
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Two teenage girls discover that mermaids really do exist after a violent storm washes one ashore. The mermaid, a sassy creature named Aquamarine, is determined to prove to her father that real love exists, and enlists the girls' help in winning the heart of a handsome lifeguard.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,482,669 (40.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.49 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:80.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,512 opening theaters/2,538 max. theaters, 3.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $32,112,347

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 3rd, 2006 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
Video Release: June 13th, 2006 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for mild language and sensuality
Running Time: 104 minutes
Plot point: Anxiety and Phobias, Arranged Marriage, Coming of Age, Culture Clash, First Love, Mermaids, Orphan, Relationship Advice, Secret Magic
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Target audience: Tween
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
